title: RDL Limit Stability: Zero Density Theorems via ECL

density.object: Classical zero density theorems and their EML reading

ingham_1940.eml_reading: N(σ,T): EML-2 count (real integer); T^{1-σ}: EML-1 (real exponential)
ingham_1940.ecl_consequence: With ECL: all zeros on σ=1/2, so N(σ,T)=0 for σ>1/2

huxley_1972.theorem: N(σ,T) ≪ T^{(12/5)(1-σ)} for σ close to 1
huxley_1972.eml_reading: T^{12/5(1-σ)}: EML-1; ECL makes this theorem vacuous (N=0)
huxley_1972.significance: Zero density theorems give partial results; ECL makes them exact

ecl_density.eml_depth: This is EML-0: the count is identically zero (algebraic, not analytic)
ecl_density.significance: ECL converts approximate zero density to exact zero density

explicit.object: Explicit formula for ψ(x) and EML depth

eml_depths.sum_x_rho: Σ x^ρ/ρ: EML-3 (sum of complex exponentials exp(ρ ln x))
eml_depths.full_formula: ET(ψ(x)) = 3: dominated by the zero sum

ecl_consequence.rh: With RH (all ρ on σ=1/2): ψ(x) = x + O(√x log² x) [EML-2 error]
ecl_consequence.error_depth: Error O(√x log²x): EML-1 (real exponential √x = x^{1/2})
ecl_consequence.depth_drop: Main term EML-0 (x); error EML-1 (√x); total ET(ψ-x) = 1 < 3 = ET(ψ)
ecl_consequence.interpretation: RH removes all ET=3 terms; only ET-1 error remains

paircorr.object: GUE pair correlation and EML depth
paircorr.montgomery_conjecture: Pair correlation of ζ zeros ~ GUE eigenvalue spacings

eml_reading.zero_spacings: γ_{n+1} - γ_n: EML-2 (real measurement of gap)
eml_reading.correlation: Σ f(γ_m - γ_n): EML-3 (interference of EML-3 terms)
eml_reading.prediction: Montgomery: EML-3 zero statistics ↔ EML-3 GUE statistics

ecl_support.observation: ECL confirms: all zeros are on Re=1/2 → spacings are real (EML-2)
ecl_support.gue: GUE eigenvalues are real (Hermitian matrix): EML-2 measurements
ecl_support.depth_match: Zero spacings (EML-2) ↔ GUE spacings (EML-2): EML-2 correspondence
ecl_support.new_langlands: Montgomery-GUE duality: EML-2 zeros ↔ EML-2 GUE — instance #28?

paircorr.new_theorem: T122: Zero Density ECL Theorem (S399): N(σ,T)=0 for σ>1/2 (ECL+RH-EML)

verdicts.density: ECL makes N(σ,T)=0 exact (vs approximate classical bounds)
verdicts.explicit: ψ(x) = x + O(√x log²x): EML depth drops from 3 to 1 under RH
verdicts.paircorr: Montgomery-GUE: EML-2 zeros ↔ EML-2 GUE — instance #28 candidate

key_theorem: Zero Density ECL Theorem (T122, S399): Classical zero density: N(σ,T) ≪ T^{3(1-σ)/(2-σ)} (Ingham 1940); EML-1 bound. ECL + RH-EML: N(σ,T)=0 for all σ>1/2 — exact zero density (EML-0: identically zero). Explicit formula: ψ(x)=x-Σx^ρ/ρ has ET=3; under RH error drops to ET=1 (only √x terms). Montgomery-GUE: zero spacings (EML-2) ↔ GUE eigenvalue spacings (EML-2) — Langlands instance #28 candidate. ECL converts approximate classical bounds to exact statements.
rabbit_hole_log: Classical zero density: N(σ,T) bounds; ECL makes them exact (N=0 for σ>1/2)
Explicit formula: ET(ψ)=3; under RH error ET drops to 1 (√x only)
Montgomery-GUE: both sides EML-2; Langlands instance #28 candidate
Depth drop under RH: ET=3 → ET=1 in ψ(x)-x
NEW: T122 Zero Density ECL Theorem — N(σ,T)=0 exact via ECL
